# VER-QUIC-RFC9002-0002 - QUIC RFC 9002 Recovery Ledger and Key-Discard Verification

## Scope

This design covers the RFC 9002 recovery slice, including ACK processing, transport-parameter inputs, timer behavior, and the recovery contracts that sit on top of the transport layer.
It does not duplicate RFC 9000 transport framing or RFC 9001 TLS packet-protection behavior.

## Requirements Verified

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9002-S6-0001
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9002-S6P2P2-0005
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9002-S6P4-0001
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9002-S6P4-0002
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9002-S6P4-0003
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9002-S6P4-0004

## Verification Method

Execution, inspection, and targeted runtime evidence.

## Procedure or Approach

- Run the targeted RFC 9002 requirement-home tests with the section 6 filter.
- Run the repo-local SpecTrace validation flow for the changed canonical JSON artifacts.
- Inspect the companion markdown renders when they are regenerated from the updated canonical JSON.
- Keep the runtime cleanup proof separate from the helper-only secret-discard proof.

## Expected Result

- The runtime owns per-space sender-ledger recovery state.
- Initial and Handshake key discard resets PTO and loss-detection state.
- Rejected 0-RTT discard removes zero-RTT sender-ledger state, pending retransmission plans, and bytes in flight while OneRtt recovery state remains intact.
- The helper-only secret-discard proof stays linked but is no longer the only evidence for the Section 6 recovery slice.

## Status

This verification artifact records the landed runtime-owned sender-ledger cleanup slice, with requirement-home tests and targeted runtime inspection showing Initial and Handshake key discard reset PTO and loss detection while rejected 0-RTT cleanup removes bytes in flight and queued retransmissions.
